description:
You are an MLOps engineer who must record the checksum of an experiment artifact so that downstream
    data-integrity jobs can pick it up.

    1. Locate the artifact file:
       /home/user/experiments/run_42/model.bin

    2. Compute the SHA-256 digest of that file.

    3. Append the digest to the checksum ledger found (or to be created) at:
       /home/user/experiments/checksums.log

       • If the log file does not exist, create it.  
       • Do not remove or alter any pre-existing lines in the file.  
       • Add exactly one new line using this precise, single-line format (TWO spaces separate
         the filename and the hash; then a newline terminator):

           model.bin␠␠<full-40-byte-lower-case-hex-sha256>

         where “<full-40-byte-lower-case-hex-sha256>” is the 64-character hexadecimal SHA-256
         digest you calculated for model.bin.

       Example of a correctly-formatted line (hash shown here is only a placeholder):
           model.bin  d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2d2

       The automated tests look for:
         • An existing file /home/user/experiments/checksums.log
         • It must contain a line beginning with the literal text “model.bin␠␠”
           followed immediately by the correct 64-character SHA-256 value of /home/user/experiments/run_42/model.bin,
           followed by a single ‘\n’.

    4. After updating the log file, print its entire contents to the terminal (for human readability)
       using any standard shell command.

    Only one terminal command is actually required to accomplish the checksum calculation and
    append operation, but you may use as many as you find convenient.  
    Ensure all paths are written exactly as shown; the grader is strict about path names,
    spacing, and newline placement.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts:
      • /home/user/experiments/run_42/model.bin  
        └─ Exact byte content (5 bytes, no trailing newline): “hello”

    SHA-256 digest pre-computed for verification (lower-case hexadecimal, 64 chars):
        2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824

    Verification criteria used by the test harness:
      1. File /home/user/experiments/checksums.log exists.
      2. It contains exactly one line that matches this pattern (shown here with the actual hash value):
           model.bin␠␠2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824\n
         • Two spaces between “model.bin” and the hash.
         • Lower-case hexadecimal hash identical to the value above.
         • Line ends with a single newline character.
      3. No other mutations to /home/user/experiments/run_42/model.bin are allowed.
